frameworks.name: GDPR
frameworks.description: Summarized GDPR security and accountability control objectives relevant to AuthClaw gateway, audit, and cloud finding evidence.
frameworks.license_note: Public EU regulation reference. AuthClaw stores internal summaries, not full legal text.

controls.title: Security of Processing
controls.summary: Personal data processing should be protected with appropriate technical and organizational safeguards, including confidentiality, integrity, availability, and resilience controls.

requirements.summary: Maintain security telemetry and audit evidence for systems that process personal data.
requirements.evidence_expectation: Gateway audit logs, policy events, and connector findings showing logging and monitoring posture.

requirements.summary: Limit access to personal data and related systems to authorized identities.
requirements.evidence_expectation: RBAC configuration, API key expiry, IAM findings, and access-control findings.

controls.title: Accountability and Data Minimization
controls.summary: Processing should be accountable, limited to necessary data, and supported by evidence of policy enforcement.

requirements.summary: Detect, redact, or block personal data exposure according to configured tenant policy.
requirements.evidence_expectation: PII/PHI detection policy, redaction events, and policy violation records.

frameworks.name: HIPAA Security Rule
frameworks.description: Summarized HIPAA Security Rule safeguards relevant to ePHI protection, access control, audit controls, and transmission security.
frameworks.license_note: Public HHS summary reference. AuthClaw stores internal summaries, not full regulatory text.

controls.title: Audit Controls
controls.summary: Systems handling ePHI should maintain mechanisms that record and examine activity in relevant information systems.

requirements.summary: Capture policy, access, and security events for systems in scope.
requirements.evidence_expectation: AuthClaw audit log entries, gateway events, and connector scan events.

controls.title: Access Control
controls.summary: Access to systems and data in scope should be limited to authorized users and reviewed through technical controls.

requirements.summary: Use tenant roles, expiring credentials, and provider IAM evidence to assess access-control posture.
requirements.evidence_expectation: RBAC state, API key expiry status, IAM findings, and integration metadata.

frameworks.name: SOC 2 Trust Services Criteria
frameworks.description: Internal summarized control objectives inspired by SOC 2 Trust Services categories for security, availability, confidentiality, processing integrity, and privacy.
frameworks.license_note: SOC 2 criteria are not copied. AuthClaw stores internal summaries and requires licensed review before using official wording.

controls.title: Logical Access Protection
controls.summary: Systems should protect information and services from unauthorized access using identity, authentication, authorization, and monitoring controls.

requirements.summary: Cloud and source-control findings related to public exposure, excessive permissions, or leaked credentials should map to access risk.
requirements.evidence_expectation: AWS/GitHub/GCP findings, RBAC configuration, API key status, and audit events.

controls.title: Monitoring and Auditability
controls.summary: Security-relevant activity should be logged, monitored, and available for review.

requirements.summary: Cloud audit logging and AuthClaw audit records should support investigation and control review.
requirements.evidence_expectation: CloudTrail/logging findings, AuthClaw audit hash chain status, and gateway event records.

frameworks.name: AuthClaw AI Governance
frameworks.description: AuthClaw internal AI governance control objectives for safe AI gateway operations, policy enforcement, human approval, and evidence-backed agent behavior.
frameworks.license_note: Internal project-plan-derived summaries. No external licensed text.

controls.title: AI Gateway Policy Enforcement
controls.summary: AI requests and responses should be evaluated against tenant policy before unsafe content or sensitive data is allowed through.

requirements.summary: PII/PHI detection and redaction/blocking should be active where tenant policy requires it.
requirements.evidence_expectation: Policy rules, gateway events, redaction outcomes, and policy violation records.

controls.title: Human Approval for Risky Actions
controls.summary: Agent-generated remediation plans should require tenant-authorized human review before execution.

requirements.summary: High-impact generated actions should be queued as expiring, non-transferable approvals.
requirements.evidence_expectation: Approval records, requester identity, expiry status, and audit events.

frameworks.name: ISO/IEC 27001
frameworks.description: Internal summarized ISO/IEC 27001-aligned information security management objectives relevant to AuthClaw evidence and cloud findings.
frameworks.license_note: ISO standard text is licensed and not copied. AuthClaw stores internal summaries only.

controls.title: Access Control Management
controls.summary: Access to information systems should be governed, limited, and reviewed based on business and security requirements.

requirements.summary: Cloud IAM, repository access, and AuthClaw RBAC evidence should support access-control review.
requirements.evidence_expectation: IAM findings, GitHub access findings, AuthClaw roles, and API key posture.

controls.title: Logging and Monitoring
controls.summary: Security-relevant events should be logged, protected, and reviewed to support detection and investigation.

requirements.summary: Audit logs and cloud logging controls should be available and protected from unauthorized alteration.
requirements.evidence_expectation: AuthClaw audit hash chain, CloudTrail/logging findings, and connector scan history.
